# OIDCLD

Compact guidance for AI systems working with this repository.

## Project

- OIDCLD is a local development edge platform that combines:
  - an OIDC / EntraID-compatible identity provider
  - managed local TLS / certificate-authority tooling
  - reverse proxy, static hosting, OpenAPI mock APIs, and a developer console
- It started as a fake OpenID Connect IdP and is still for development / testing only.
- It is not a production identity provider, reverse proxy, or certificate authority.

## Capabilities

- Authorization Code Flow
- Client Credentials Flow
- Device Flow
- Refresh token support
- RP-initiated logout support
- OIDC discovery and JWKS endpoints
- Optional EntraID-compatible issuer and claim shape modes
- In-memory users with click-to-login UX instead of passwords
- Optional `/login` environment banner and Markdown info panel
- Managed local development CA with downloadable root certificate and install / uninstall scripts
- Shared HTTPS termination for `oidc.localhost`, `app.localhost`, and `app2.localhost`
- Virtual host and path routing via `reverse_proxy.hosts[]`
- Upstream proxying for frontend and API routes
- Static hosting with SPA fallback
- OpenAPI-backed mock responses
- Reverse-proxy request logs in the Developer Console

## Commands

- `oidcld init`
  - Generates `oidcld.yaml` from `standard`, `entraid-v1`, or `entraid-v2` templates.

- `oidcld serve`
  - Default HTTP port: `8080`
  - Default HTTPS port: `8443`
  - In HTTPS mode, `--http-readonly-port` defaults to `8080` and exposes discovery, JWKS, and health only.
  - `--proxy-port` optionally splits reverse-proxy traffic onto a dedicated browser-facing listener.

- `oidcld health`
  - If `--url` is omitted, it auto-detects the target from config.
  - In container-style setups where `OIDCLD_CONFIG` is set, health checks dial `localhost` and allow self-signed / local TLS.

- `oidcld mcp`
  - Starts the MCP server mode.

## Runtime

- Default config file name: `oidcld.yaml`
- `serve` enables a local access filter by default
- Requests without forwarded headers must come from loopback or RFC1918 / local private ranges
- Requests with `Forwarded` or `X-Forwarded-For` are rejected unless `access_filter.max_forwarded_hops` is raised
- Signing keys are generated in memory at startup and are not persisted
- Tokens from a previous run will not validate after restart
- HTTPS can be provided by manual TLS files, `autocert`, or the managed development CA path
- When `--proxy-port` is used, OIDC and reverse-proxy listeners can choose HTTP or HTTPS independently

## Config

Primary file: `oidcld.yaml`

Current top-level sections:

1. `access_filter`
2. `oidc`
3. `console`
4. `certificate_authority`
5. `entraid`
6. `autocert`
7. `reverse_proxy`
8. `users`

```yaml
access_filter:
  enabled: true
  extra_allowed_ips: []
  max_forwarded_hops: 0

oidc:
  iss: "https://oidc.localhost:8443"
  pkce_required: true
  nonce_required: true
  expired_in: 3600
  aud_claim_format: "string"
  valid_scopes:
    - "openid"
    - "profile"
    - "email"
    - "offline_access"
    - "User.Read"
  login_ui:
    env_title: "Compose Sample"
    accent_color: "#2563EB"
    info_markdown_file: "./login-info.md"
  refresh_token_enabled: true
  refresh_token_expiry: 86400
  end_session_enabled: true
  end_session_endpoint_visible: true
  verbose_logging: false
  tls_cert_file: ""
  tls_key_file: ""
  cors: true

console:
  bind_address: "127.0.0.1"

certificate_authority:
  ca_dir: "./tls"
  domains:
    - "oidc.localhost"
    - "app.localhost"
    - "app2.localhost"
  ca_cert_ttl: "87600h"
  leaf_cert_ttl: "720h"

entraid:
  tenant_id: "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc"
  version: "v2"

autocert:
  enabled: false
  domains: []
  email: ""
  agree_tos: false
  cache_dir: "./autocert-cache"
  acme_server: ""
  staging: false
  renewal_threshold: 1

reverse_proxy:
  log_retention: 200
  ignore_log_paths:
    - "/health"
  hosts:
    - host: "https://app.localhost"
      cors: true
      routes:
        - path: "/api"
          target_url: "http://127.0.0.1:3000"
        - path: "/"
          target_url: "http://127.0.0.1:8080"
    - host: "https://app2.localhost"
      cors: true
      routes:
        - path: "/apimock"
          openapi_file: "./openapi/mock.yaml"
        - path: "/"
          static_dir: "./dist"
          spa_fallback: true

users:
  admin:
    display_name: "Administrator"
    extra_valid_scopes:
      - "User.Read"
    extra_claims:
      email: "admin@example.com"
      role: "admin"
```

- `oidc` is the IdP section; old top-level `oidcld` is legacy and rejected
- `access_filter` is top-level in 0.2; it is no longer nested under the IdP section
- top-level `cors` is legacy and rejected; use `oidc.cors` or `reverse_proxy.hosts[].cors`
- `aud_claim_format` controls whether a single `aud` claim is emitted as a string or array
- `login_ui` affects the `/login` page only
- `certificate_authority.domains` covers hosts for managed development certificates
- `reverse_proxy.hosts[]` is the virtual-host table; each route must choose exactly one of `target_url`, `static_dir`, or `openapi_file`
- In split listener mode, all explicit `reverse_proxy.hosts[].host` values must use one scheme and any explicit host port must match `--proxy-port`

## Compose

The repository root `compose.yaml` defines a full integrated sample:

- `app2-static-builder`
  - Builds a second frontend bundle for `app2.localhost`
  - Writes static output to the `app2-static-dist` volume

- `oidc.localhost`
  - Builds OIDCLD from the current repository
  - Binds `./examples/reverseproxy/config` into the container read-only
  - Mounts `app2-static-dist` for static hosting
  - Mounts `oidcld-managed-ca` for persistent local CA state
  - Exposes `8443:443` for the shared HTTPS listener used by `oidc.localhost`, `app.localhost`, and `app2.localhost`
  - Exposes `8888:8888` for the Developer Console and HTTP metadata companion listener
  - Starts with `serve --config /app/config/oidcld.yaml --port 443`
  - Health check uses `oidcld health --url http://localhost:8888`

- `app-frontend`
  - Sample frontend container built from `examples/azure-msal-browser-react`
  - Is reachable through OIDCLD at `https://app.localhost:8443`

- `demo-api`
  - Sample backend container for `/api/*`

- Browser-facing TLS traffic:
  - `https://oidc.localhost:8443` for the issuer
  - `https://app.localhost:8443` for the reverse-proxied frontend and `/api/*`
  - `https://app2.localhost:8443` for static hosting and `/apimock/*`

- Plain HTTP on `http://localhost:8888` is the Developer Console plus metadata companion, not a full plaintext app surface

## Environment

Environment variables currently consumed by the implementation:

- `OIDCLD_VERBOSE`
  - Enables verbose logging for `serve`

- `OIDCLD_CONFIG`
  - Used as a runtime convention in container examples
  - Important for `health` auto-detection behavior in container-style setups

- `OIDCLD_ENV_TITLE`
  - Overrides `oidc.login_ui.env_title`

- `OIDCLD_ENV_COLOR`
  - Overrides `oidc.login_ui.accent_color`

- `OIDCLD_ENV_MARKDOWN_FILE`
  - Overrides `oidc.login_ui.info_markdown_file`

- `OIDCLD_ACME_DIRECTORY_URL`
  - Overrides `autocert.acme_server`

- `OIDCLD_ACME_EMAIL`
  - Overrides `autocert.email`

- `OIDCLD_ACME_DOMAIN`
  - Comma-separated domain list for `autocert.domains`

- `OIDCLD_ACME_CACHE_DIR`
  - Overrides `autocert.cache_dir`

- `OIDCLD_ACME_AGREE_TOS`
  - Enables Terms of Service agreement for autocert when set to `true`

Documented in repository docs but not directly read by the current Go entrypoints:

- `PORT`
  - Prefer explicit CLI flags such as `oidcld serve --port ...`

Present in older examples or docs but not parsed by the current `internal/config.LoadConfig` implementation:

- `OIDCLD_ACME_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY`
- `OIDCLD_ACME_RENEWAL_THRESHOLD`

## Source Of Truth

- `README.md`
- `README.ja.md`
- `COMPATIBILITY.md`
- `docs/config.md`
- `compose.yaml`
- `internal/config/config.go`
- `main.go`
- `serve.go`

## Guidance Constraints

- Do not present OIDCLD as production-ready
- Do not assume persistent signing keys, client registration, or redirect URI allowlists
- Do not assume every environment variable seen in old docs or examples is implemented
- When explaining 0.2 config, use `oidc`, top-level `access_filter`, and non-legacy CORS placement
- When explaining HTTPS mode, mention the restricted HTTP metadata companion listener
- When explaining Compose, describe OIDCLD as both IdP and edge router
- When discussing migration, point to `COMPATIBILITY.md`
